t_ch: 关于「奇美拉世界锦标赛」…
t_en: About the Chimera World Tournament...

t_ch: 既然你诚心诚意地提问了，*我们*就大发慈悲地告诉你——
t_en: Since you asked so sincerely, we shall tell you graciously—

t_ch: 其实，举办这场「奇美拉世界锦标赛」的目的，是为了守护世界和平！
t_en: Actually, the purpose of holding this Chimera World Tournament is to protect the world from devastation!

t_ch: 什么？居然还有这个设定？
t_en: What? That's actually a thing?

t_ch: 哼哼，不知道了吧？要是不定期举办比赛，让奇美拉有事可做的话，它们就要回去工作哦。毕竟，要忙的事就像书库里的字一样多呢。
t_en: Heh-heh, bet you didn't know that, huh? If we don't hold competitions regularly to keep the chimeras busy, they'll have to go back to work. After all, there's as much work to do as there are books in the repository!

t_ch: 要是工作太久的话，奇美拉积攒的怒火就要席卷整个翁法罗斯了，很可怕的。
t_en: If the chimeras work too long, their pent-up rage will sweep through all of Amphoreus. That's terrifying.

t_ch: 所以小灰有空的时候，要多来比赛！这也是为了保护世界！
t_en: So Little Gray, you should come to compete more often when you're free! This is also to protect the world!

t_ch: …这是你现编的吧。
t_en: ...You're just making this up.

t_ch: 啊，不许拆穿！小灰没有幽默感，这种时候应该配合*我们*讲故事才对。
t_en: Ah, don't call us out like that! Little Gray has no sense of humor! At times like this, you should play along with us in telling the story!

t_ch: 算啦，闲聊就到这里吧。待会就要在赛场上碰面了哦。
t_en: All right, let's wrap up the chit-chat. We'll be meeting on the field soon anyway.

t_ch: 嗷呜！欢迎！我们的特邀嘉宾来了，这里是「奇美拉世界锦标赛」的报名点。
t_en: Awoo! Welcome! Our special guest has arrived! This is the registration point for the Chimera World Tournament!

t_ch: 但比赛还没有开始，先在花园里随便逛逛吧。
t_en: The competition hasn't started yet, so why not take a stroll around the garden first?

t_ch: 听说这次的「十三大师」啊…
t_en: I heard about the "Thirteen Masters" this time...

t_ch: 上吧，华丽地击败对手！
t_en: Let's go! Take them down in style!

t_ch: 好讨厌的感觉…
t_en: Ugh... what an awful feeling...

t_ch: 啧，你们多洛斯人就没有偷以外的打招呼方式？没错，树庭的地区徽章就在我手里。
t_en: Tsk, don't you Dolosians have any way to say hello besides stealing? Yes, I have the Grove's regional badge right here.

t_ch: 想带走的话，先想办法通过我的测验吧。
t_en: If you want to take it, you'll have to pass my test first.

t_ch: 我说啊，树庭男孩，你是什么时候产生了能打得过我的错觉？嘻，究竟谁是考官……
t_en: Hey, Grove boy, when exactly did you get the delusion that you could beat me? Heh, who do you think is the examiner here...

t_ch: 不到最后，可都还说不准哦？
t_en: Nothing's set in stone until the very end, you know?

t_ch: 奇美拉对战的具体规则，就在实战中做讲解好啦。我们现在就开始吧。
t_en: I'll explain the specific rules of chimera battles during the actual combat. Let's get started right now.

t_ch: 好哦。小灰，和*我们*一起，成为奇美拉训练家吧！
t_en: All right! Little Gray, join us and become a chimera trainer!

t_ch: 去吧！奇美拉！
t_en: Go, chimera!

t_ch: （「奇美拉世界锦标赛」好像还有新的赛事活动，有空的时候再去看看吧。）
t_en: (Looks like there are new events in the "Chimera World Tournament." I should check them out when I have time.)

t_ch: 灰宝，为我应援吧！
t_en: Cheer for me, Grayie!

t_ch: 小奇美拉乖乖，把门开开~
t_en: Little chimera be a good kid and open the door~

t_ch: 对面看起来就好弱，你也不想输给它们吧？
t_en: The opposition looks so weak. You wouldn't want to lose to them, right?

t_ch: 听。鱼儿们在吐泡泡呢。
t_en: Listen. The fish are blowing bubbles.

t_ch: 奇美拉的字典里，没有「失败」二字。
t_en: The word "failure" does not exist in the chimera language.

t_ch: 不必担忧，我会捍卫这份荣誉。
t_en: Don't worry. I will defend this honor.

t_ch: 听话~吃饱了才有力气战斗呀。
t_en: Be good~ You can't fight on an empty stomach.

t_ch: 认真些，可别让你身披的金织蒙羞。
t_en: Take this seriously. Don't bring shame to the woven gold you're adorned with.

t_ch: 奇兽爵，令对手臣服于我吧。
t_en: Dux Chimera, make our opponent submit to me.

t_ch: 你们是我教过的奇美拉里，最优秀的一届。
t_en: Among all the chimeras I've instructed, you are the most outstanding.

t_ch: 不管是金币还是奇美拉，我全都要~
t_en: I want both the money and the chimera~!

t_ch: 我要成为最强的奇美拉训练家！
t_en: I want to become the best chimera trainer!

t_ch: 小小一只的奇美拉，快快在爱里长大吧♪
t_en: Little chimera, grow up quickly, nurtured by love♪

t_ch: 仿品？哼！你知道它能追到什么年代吗？至少是{RUBY_B#命运圣城}雅努萨波利斯{RUBY_E#}尚在的时候！
t_en: Replica? Do you know how far back it dates? It's from at least the time when the {RUBY_B#Holy City of Fate}Janusopolis{RUBY_E#} was still standing!

t_ch: 这是我曾曾曾祖父在那儿当祭司时，命运三泰坦赐予他的，在我家放了几千年，这能有假吗？
t_en: This was bestowed upon my great-great-great-grandfather while he served as a priest there by the Three Titans of Fate, and it's been in my family for thousands of years. How could it be fake?

t_ch: 大爷，您先冷静，听我再说一遍——您这件是新的，毫无争议的新，上周刚造的那种新。
t_en: Sir, please calm down, and let me say this again — your item is undeniably new, freshly made last week.

t_ch: 臭小子，我看你才是新的！
t_en: Kid, you're the new one!

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 你骗人家养老金？
t_en: Are you scamming pensions?

t_ch: 您碰瓷？
t_en: Are you faking an injury?

t_ch: 哎，最近奥赫玛出现了一大批采用最新仿造技术制成的假古董，不光是辨别能力较弱的老人，甚至连一些专业爱好者也难以分清。
t_en: Recently, Okhema has seen a surge of fakes made with the latest imitation techniques, not just fooling the elderly with weak discernment but even some enthusiasts.

t_ch: 你骂谁老？你知道它的同款罐子拍了多少钱吗？两百万利衡币！你就是打算压低价格，再转手高价卖咯，狡猾的二手贩子！
t_en: Who are you calling old? Do you have any idea what its twin pot fetched? Two million Balance Coins! You're just trying to lower the price so you can flip it later for a huge profit, you sly second-hand dealer!

t_ch: 这破罐子值这么多钱？
t_en: Is this pot worth that much?

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: Exactly!

t_ch: 这样吧，我来仔细鉴定下。不过您要做好准备，结果不会有任何不同。
t_en: Allow me to conduct a thorough appraisal. But be prepared, the result will be no different.

t_ch: 这位客人，您在这听了这么久，是对鉴宝感兴趣？
t_en: Dear guest, you've been observing for a while now. Are you interested in learning about artifact appraisal?

t_ch: 正好，借此机会我可以为您演示一番，只是做宝物鉴定需要些特殊的材料。他老人家临时造访，我手头也没准备，要是你正巧带着的话，可就帮大忙了。
t_en: This is a perfect opportunity for a demonstration. Appraising treasures requires certain specific materials. The man's visit was unexpected, and I wasn't prepared. If you happen to have those materials with you, it would be a tremendous help.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 你们两个在偷偷摸摸搞什么？我警告你，休想调包我的宝贝！
t_en: What are you two mumbling about? Don't get any ideas about swapping out my treasure!

t_ch: 快点让他死心吧。
t_en: Enough already.

t_ch: 鉴宝大师来了！
t_en: The master appraiser is here!

t_ch: 哼，把我的鉴定费还我，不然我上圣城卫士那儿告你去！
t_en: Refund my appraisal fee, or I'll report you to the Holy City Guard!

t_ch: 您拿好了，下次要记得——几百块，可买不到两百万的宝贝！
t_en: Here's a reminder — a few hundred bucks won't get you a treasure worth two million!

t_ch: 出不起钱就别开古玩店，我找别人收去。
t_en: If you can't handle the costs, maybe running an antique shop isn't for you. I'll find a more serious buyer.

t_ch: 如你所见，由于近期持续动乱，奥赫玛内假货猖獗，每天都有鉴定不完的假宝贝。
t_en: As you can see, due to the recent unrest, Okhema is rampant with forgeries, leaving us with an endless stream of fakes to appraise every day.

t_ch: 如果你也想学两手鉴定之术，不妨每天来我这儿帮帮忙，我也会将店内收益分给你做报酬的。
t_en: If you're keen on mastering the art of appraisal, why not lend a hand at my shop every day? In return, I'll cut you in on the profits.

t_ch: 还有这种好事？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 好吧，既然你都拜托我了。
t_en: Alright, since you've asked.

t_ch: 嗯，我还可以顺带给你介绍下这些宝贝背后的翁法罗斯史，可谓是一举三得。光说话也没意思，先来看看我手头还有些什么吧。
t_en: I can also give you a quick rundown of the history behind these treasures, three birds with one stone. But enough chatting, let's take a look at what else I've got here.

t_ch: 查看待鉴定宝物。
t_en: Check the treasures waiting for appraisal.

t_ch: 你学得挺快的，明天要继续来进修喔。
t_en: You're a quick learner. Be sure to come back tomorrow for more training.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 关于学习鉴宝…
t_en: About learning appraisal...

t_ch: 你可算是来了…这盆也不知经历了什么，臭气熏天的，熏得客人都不敢进店。快点鉴定完，送它物归原主吧。
t_en: You're finally here... This pot's been through who knows what, and it reeks so badly it's driving away customers. Let's get it appraised quickly and send it back to its owner.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 手持珍宝的贵客漫不经心，怀揣赝品的愚人反当成宝。如此有历史沉淀的物件，却被溺爱的主人拿来做奇美拉砂盆。
t_en: How sad that the esteemed guests with genuine treasures treat their gems with nonchalance, while a fool buying counterfeits thinks their hoard is priceless. I can't believe that such a historically valuable item is being used by someone to spoil their pet chimera as a litter box.

t_ch: 这宝贝什么来头？
t_en: What's the story of this treasure?

t_ch: 奇兽一直是翁法罗斯器皿上的常见主题。许多远古的物种都消失了，我们唯有透过前人的勾勒去想象它们的模样——
t_en: Magical beasts have always been a common theme on Amphoreus vessels. Many ancient species have vanished, and all we can do is imagine their forms through the sketches of our ancestors —

t_ch: 能喷出太阳之火的雄狮，与月亮相伴的飞鸟…它们是神话中的生物吗，还是真实存在过？我也想知道啊。
t_en: Lions that could breathe the fire of the sun, birds that accompanied the moon... Were these creatures of myth, or did they exist? I'd like to know that too.

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。
t_en: See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 嗯，明天也要来进修啊。
t_en: And yes, come for more training tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天这位大爷送来的东西不得了，他儿子知道他喜欢音乐，送了他一把黄金里拉琴。这样的宝贝可不是天天有，让你开开眼界！
t_en: Today brought an extraordinary surprise — a gentleman received a golden lyre from his son, who knows well his father's love for music. Such treasures are rare indeed. Feast your eyes on this marvel!

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 我竟没有觉察到其中的问题。都怪我一时太兴奋，以为可以见到传闻中难得一见的黄金里拉琴。
t_en: I failed to notice the issues in my excitement, thinking I could see the rarely seen golden lyre mentioned in rumors.

t_ch: 这宝贝什么来头？
t_en: What's the story of this treasure?

t_ch: 翁法罗斯有一个美丽的传说：相传，法吉娜是世间最好的琴师，海洋造物听到她的琴声会变得温和，风浪听到她的琴声亦会平息。
t_en: Amphoreus has a beautiful legend: It's said that Phagousa was the best lyre player in the world. The ocean calmed, and the winds stilled at the sound of her music.

t_ch: 纯金制成的里拉琴最接近她的琴声，航海士们奏响它以祈求出海平安。但在法吉娜沉默后，这招对于悲伤的大海就不管用了。
t_en: A lyre made of pure gold comes closest to replicating her music, played by seafarers to pray for safe voyages. But after Phagousa went mute, this tactic no longer worked on the sorrowful sea.

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。
t_en: See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 嗯，明天也要来进修啊。
t_en: And yes, come for more training tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 自从知道我这儿来了个新鉴宝师，城中的大家便送了不少怪东西给你练手。{RUBY_B#餐厅老板}克瑞斯{RUBY_E#}拿来的「宝贝」，你看看吧。
t_en: Ever since they knew a new appraiser had come to town, everyone has been sending all sorts of strange things for you to practice with. The {RUBY_B#restaurant owner}Kyros{RUBY_E#} brought this "treasure." Take a look.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 他还把这块肉当宝贝一样，供在餐厅正中央让顾客参观，说这是神造万物的直接证据。
t_en: He treasures this piece of meat as a rare artifact, prominently displayed at the center of his restaurant for all to see, touted as proof of divine creation.

t_ch: 这宝贝什么来头？
t_en: What's the story of this treasure?

t_ch: 依照克瑞斯的鬼话，这是世界上第一头角猪的前腿肉腌制的火腿，因为它不带有生物成长的痕迹，诞生既是成年体。
t_en: Kyros claims this is a preserved ham from the foreleg of the world's first hornswine, which, according to his fantastic tales, was born fully matured without a trace of natural growth.

t_ch: 若生物有生长痕迹，证明它由幼体发育而来；若它生来就是成熟的模样，则是神的原初造物，例如没有纹路的贝壳、见不到年轮的树木，都受到某些人的追捧。
t_en: If an organism bears marks of growth, it's a sign that it evolved from a youthful form. Conversely, if it's mature from its inception, it's regarded as a divine creation, like seamless shells or ageless trees — objects of desire for many.

t_ch: 把它当个故事听吧，你只要记得这块火腿不符合奥赫玛食品卫生标准就好。
t_en: Consider it merely a tall tale, and keep in mind that this ham falls short of Okhema's food safety regulations.

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。
t_en: See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 嗯，明天也要来进修啊。
t_en: And yes, come for more training tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 你来得正是时候。今早刚开门，有个人急忙送来这杯子，还支付了三倍鉴定费加急，也不知道在急什么……
t_en: Your timing couldn't be better. Right after opening this morning, someone hurried in with this cup, paying triple the usual fee for an urgent appraisal. I'm still puzzled by the rush.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 这这这！偷人家的宝贝，还想在我这儿换成钱逃之夭夭，没门！我得赶紧联系圣城卫士，不然到时候进去的就是我了。
t_en: No way! Do you think you can steal a treasure and cash it out here without consequence? I need to call the Holy City Guard right now — before I end up behind bars myself.

t_ch: 这宝贝什么来头？
t_en: What's the story of this treasure?

t_ch: 这杯子原本成对，传说是法吉娜与吉奥里亚对饮时，为两位神明所用。后来两位泰坦因管辖权势不两立，愤怒的吉奥里亚砸碎了它的那只，以回应法吉娜的挑衅。
t_en: Originally part of a set, this cup was used by the deities Phagousa and Georios during their divine libations. As tensions between the Titans escalated over territorial disputes, in a fit of rage, Georios shattered his cup, a stark response to Phagousa's provocations.

t_ch: 仅存的这一只属于法吉娜，千年来一直是城中某家族的私人藏品。
t_en: The surviving cup, once belonging to Phagousa, has been kept within a family's private collection in the city for thousands of years.

t_ch: 数百年前有人造了一批它的仿品，也算得上有价值的宝物，我便未起疑心。没想到…这递到我手上的正是原品。哈，这种珍宝怎么会在市场上流通呢！
t_en: Centuries ago, someone produced a series of replicas that were treasures in their own right, which is why I was unsuspecting. Yet, what ended up in my hands was the original. It's hard to believe such a treasure could ever make its way onto the market!

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。
t_en: See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 嗯，明天也要来进修啊。
t_en: And yes, come for more training tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天来得很早嘛。需要你看一个单耳水瓶，是翁法罗斯相当经典的器型。
t_en: You're early today. I have a single-eared amphora for you to check out — it's a classic piece from Amphoreus.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 哈哈，这骗子还真是引人发笑。
t_en: Ha, this scam artist is truly a joke.

t_ch: 这宝贝什么来头？
t_en: What's the story of this treasure?

t_ch: 高度融入生活中的器物，其源头往往被岁月掩埋。
t_en: Artifacts deeply integrated into daily life often have origins obscured by the passage of time.

t_ch: 有人说，这瓶口的形状像悬锋战士的头盔，因此推断它最早来自悬锋城，边沿的那抹红色则来自尼卡多利的鲜血。
t_en: Some claim the bottle's neck resembles a Kremnos Warrior's helmet, hinting at its origins in Castrum Kremnos, with the red shade at its edge a trace of Nikador's blood.

t_ch: 而我不太相信这个说法，悬锋人淬火冶金发达，应更惯用金属器皿才是。
t_en: I'm skeptical of that tale. Kremnoans, with their advanced metalworking, would more likely favor metal vessels.

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。
t_en: See you tomorrow.

t_ch: 嗯，明天也要来进修啊。
t_en: And yes, come for more training tomorrow.

t_ch: 今天没有适合你上手的宝物啦，请明天再来吧。
t_en: There are no suitable treasures for you to handle today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 哎，你说白厄大人是在戏弄我吗？这样一个云石天宫随处可见的澡盆，应当劈成柴拿去给{RUBY_B#大工匠}哈托努斯{RUBY_E#}烧了，而不是拿给我。
t_en: Ah, is Lord Phainon pulling my leg? Such a common tub, seen everywhere in the Marmoreal Palace, should be chopped for firewood for the {RUBY_B#Grand Craftsman}Chartonus{RUBY_E#}, not handed to me.

t_ch: 材料没带着吗…我这会儿不太方便走开，要麻烦你去取过来了，真不好意思。
t_en: You don't have the materials with you? I'm tied up at the moment, so you'll need to go get them. Sorry for the inconvenience.

t_ch: 这件宝贝，我得问问白厄大人是否愿意出让给我。不过，在此之前——
t_en: This treasure — I must inquire if Lord Phainon is willing to part with it. But first —

t_ch: 恭喜你，{NICKNAME}，经过七日的学习，你已经是个合格的鉴宝师了！
t_en: Congratulations, {NICKNAME}, after a week of training, you're now a certified appraiser!

t_ch: 我也能造假货了。
t_en: I can forge items myself now.

t_ch: 尽管放马过来，即使是你造的也逃不过我的眼睛，虽然我知道你不会的。
t_en: Bring it on. Even if you made it, it won't escape my scrutiny, though I know you wouldn't do that.

t_ch: 反诈骗有我一份力。
t_en: I'm doing my part in the fight against fraud.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的打假就靠你了，灭灭骗子们的气焰！
t_en: Okhema's battle against counterfeiters is in your hands! Show those fraudsters who's the boss!

t_ch: 对了，还有件谢礼要送给你——请收下这件黄金裔陶罐。我做的陶罐在奥赫玛可是一罐难求，预订单已经排到三个月后了。不过请不要告诉别人，不然要说我不公平了。
t_en: Here's a special gift for you — a Chrysos Heir Dolium. These jars are highly sought after in Okhema, and my waitlist extends three months out. But let's keep this between us, or they'll accuse me of playing favorites.

t_ch: 我会好好珍藏的。
t_en: I'll make sure to take good care of it.

t_ch: 这就拿去卖了换钱。
t_en: I'm going to sell this right away and cash in.

t_ch: 有没有我推的款？
t_en: Any recommendations?

t_ch: 哈哈，事先声明，每个陶罐绘制的图案都不相同哦，多年后全是价值连城的孤品。
t_en: Ha, just so you know, each dolium features a unique design. They'll all be priceless rarities years from now.

t_ch: 那么，我先去招呼客人了，回见。
t_en: Well, I need to greet some other customers now. See you later.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，研究古玩很容易上瘾的，有什么需要的？
t_en: Ha, studying ancient artifacts is addictive, isn't it? Anything else you need?

t_ch: 查看鉴宝记录。
t_en: Check the appraisal records.

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: I'm good.

t_ch: 有需要随时回来。
t_en: Come back anytime you need something.

t_ch: 嘿，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，你就是维德提到的「大人物」吧，星穹列车的无名客？
t_en: Hey {F#miss}{M#dude}, you must be that "big shot" Vader mentioned. A Nameless from the Astral Express, right?

t_ch: 我叫凯特，和维德一样都是猎犬家系的守卫。那小子说之前有幸见过您——呃，长话短说吧，我们俩捡到了个宝贝……
t_en: I'm Kate, a guard from the Bloodhound Family, just like Vader. He said he had the pleasure of meeting you before— Ahem, long story short, we found something real valuable...

t_ch: 捡到了宝贝，你就自己收着吧…
t_en: Found a treasure, huh? You can keep it...

t_ch: 不行，这么好的宝贝，我们不得不和您分享——
t_en: No way, this is too good not to share with you—

t_ch: …等等，维德是谁？你又是谁？
t_en: ...Wait, who's Vader? And who are you again?

t_ch: 您不记得我们也是正常的，重要的是这个宝贝——
t_en: It's fine if you don't remember us. What really matters is this treasure right here—

t_ch: 我收下了，谢谢你陌生的朋友。
t_en: I'll take it. Thanks, stranger.

t_ch: 等等，我并不是来这里给您送礼而已…您先看看这个宝贝——
t_en: Hold up, I'm not just here to give you a gift... Take a look at this treasure first—

t_ch: 是枚梦泡，叫「不要笑挑战」。这梦泡里隐藏的奇妙能量，让人忍不住想要恶作剧，想要找点乐子…以及放声大笑…非常好理解，对吧？
t_en: It's a dream bubble called "Try-Not-to-Laugh Challenge." It contains a peculiar energy that makes you want to pull pranks, seek some thrills... and just laugh your heart out... Pretty easy to understand, right?

t_ch: 这玩意恰到好处地难以处理——既找不到失物主人，也没办法贸然上报给家族，说到底…任谁也不会将它判定为「危险物」，家族只会置之不理。
t_en: The thing is, it's awkward to deal with... I can't find who it belongs to, and I can't exactly report it to The Family either. When it comes down to it... no one would ever call it "dangerous," so The Family just shrugs it off.

t_ch: …所以，我想拜托您来检查下这个梦泡。毕竟像您这样的大人物，说不定见多识广……
t_en: ...So, I was hoping you could take a look at it. After all, a big shot like you has probably seen all kinds of strange things, right...?

t_ch: 这不就是字面上的不要笑挑战？
t_en: So... a literal try-not-to-laugh challenge?

t_ch: 看搞笑内容，努力憋笑，并录下自己的反应？不不不，恰恰相反……
t_en: Watch funny stuff, try hard not to laugh, and record your reaction? No no no, quite the opposite...

t_ch: 万一它真的很危险的话怎么办？
t_en: What if it's actually dangerous?

t_ch: 嗐，对于这枚孱弱的梦泡来说，遇上了无敌的您，那是它的危险呢……
t_en: Ah well, for such a fragile dream bubble, running into someone as invincible as you... it's the one in danger...

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我们进入梦泡之后，根本笑不出来。每当有人在梦泡里战斗时，那种奇妙的能量就会变得更加活跃…我们还听到了一些隐约的憋笑声。
t_en: To be honest, we couldn't even crack a smile after entering the dream bubble. Whenever someone fights in it, that mysterious energy flares up even more... We even heard some faint, muffled giggles.

t_ch: 您明白了吗？这梦泡并不是入梦者的不要笑挑战，而是神秘人的不要笑挑战！
t_en: You see? This dream bubble isn't a try-not-to-laugh challenge for dreamers. It's the challenge for that mysterious person!

t_ch: 可恶！真是岂有此理！
t_en: How outrageous!

t_ch: 我擅自将这种能量称作「憋笑度」，我猜测，当憋笑度到达极限时，就算是梦泡里的神秘人也会…憋不住放声大笑？那就是神秘人输了！
t_en: I called this energy the Giggle Gauge. My guess is once it hits a certain limit, even that mysterious person in the dream bubble will... burst out laughing uncontrollably? That's when they lose!

t_ch: 但很可惜，我和维德太弱了。只好拜托您来试试，以您的身手，说不定能让人憋笑失败。
t_en: Unfortunately, Vader and I are too weak. That's why we're asking for your help to try it. With your skills, you might just be able to make them lose.

t_ch: 你的意思是，我是{F#搞笑女}{M#搞笑男}？
t_en: So you're saying I'm a {F#goofball}{M#goofball}?

t_ch: 当然不是，但是…怎么说呢，这其实是在说您武德充沛呀！
t_en: Of course not, but... how should I put this? What I meant is you're brimming with martial prowess!

t_ch: 我太强了，只会让他笑不出来…
t_en: I'm too strong. They won't even be able to crack up...

t_ch: 您误会了，这不是让您痛击神秘人的意思。
t_en: You misunderstood. I'm not telling you to bash the mysterious person.

t_ch: 神秘人的笑点也很神秘！我们试了好几遍，战斗上表现越出色，越能使神秘人笑出声来！
t_en: Their sense of humor is just as mysterious! We tried several times, and the better our combat performance, the more likely it is to get them burst out laughing.

t_ch: 所以就…拜托你啦？您放心，我们对危险梦泡有一整套的安全监管手段，您不会遇到任何危险…随着调查深入，您也会收到相应的报酬……
t_en: So... please help us out? Don't worry, we have a complete set of safety measures for dangerous dream bubbles, you won't face any risks. And as the investigation progresses, you'll receive compensation accordingly...

t_ch: 总之，这个梦泡就交给您了，加油！咱就回去接着上班了……
t_en: Anyway, this dream bubble's all yours now. Good luck! I'm heading back to work...

t_ch: 你好！欢迎回来喵。
t_en: Hi! Welcome back, meow!

t_ch: 还没来得及搭话，你便注意到艾丝妲身旁的桌上蹲着只你此前从未见过的造物。
t_en: Before you can even say a word, you notice an unfamiliar creature crouching on the table beside Asta.

t_ch: 而它的模样，无疑让你想起了某位故人……
t_en: And its appearance unmistakably reminds you of someone from your past...

t_ch: 好久不见呀， {NICKNAME}。欢迎回到「黑塔」空间站！
t_en: Long time no see, {NICKNAME}. Welcome back to the Herta Space Station!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我专程约在禁闭舱段见面，想必…你应该能猜到找你有什么事了吧？
t_en: Hehe, I specifically arranged to meet you in the seclusion zone... I'm sure you can guess why I called you here?

t_ch: 我知道了，你想找我借钱。
t_en: I see, you want to borrow money.

t_ch: 啊，不会不会。虽说管理黑塔女士带回来的奇物，确实给空间站造成了不小的预算压力，但放心，资金方面的窟窿，我自己就能填上。
t_en: Ah, no, no. While managing the Curios that Madam Herta brings back has put quite a strain on the space station's budget, rest assured, I can cover those financial gaps myself.

t_ch: 能用信用点解决的问题，当然不算是问题。
t_en: Problems that can be resolved with credits are never problems.

t_ch: …不会要抓我去做实验吧！
t_en: ...Don't tell me you're going to use me as a lab rat!

t_ch: 嗯？听起来像是黑塔女士会做的事，我提出的委托…至多就是陪佩佩玩会吧。
t_en: Hmm? That sounds like something Madam Herta would do. As for my assignment... at most, it's just playing with Peppy for a bit.

t_ch: 开玩笑的。佩佩已经是很成熟的科员了，这种小事不需要麻烦你。
t_en: Just kidding. Peppy's already a very capable researcher, no need to trouble you with such small matters.

t_ch: 莫非…和造物们有关系？
t_en: Could it be... related to the creations?

t_ch: 答对啦。就是阮•梅女士留在空间站的这群小生命，和你也算是颇有渊源呢。
t_en: Correct! These little lifeforms that Miss Ruan Mei left at the space station have quite a history with you, after all.

t_ch: 我就不卖关子了，事情是这样的。阮•梅女士为小家伙们设计了一组新的训练项目，但她最近抽不开身，所以就把主导工作交给了我们。
t_en: Let me cut to the chase. Here's the deal. Miss Ruan Mei designed a new set of training programs for the little ones, but she's been too busy lately, so she handed over the lead role to us.

t_ch: 喵。都说不是「训练」，是大家共同举办的「赛跑」，是自发的比赛！
t_en: Meow. I told you it's not "training," it's a "race" we're all holding together. A spontaneous competition!

t_ch: 也不需要助手，只用在观众席为我们应援，呐喊助威就够，我们自己会跑快快的喵。
t_en: We don't need any assistant, just cheer for us from the stands! Your support is all we need, and we'll run super fast on our own, meow!

t_ch: 支持造物的合法权利！
t_en: Support the legal rights of creations!

t_ch: 反对动物表演！
t_en: Say no to animal shows!

t_ch: 哈哈，我先做个整体说明吧。这项训练…该怎么形容呢，确实像是场<color=#dbc291ff>造物们的定向越野赛</color>。
t_en: Haha, let me give you an overview first. This training... how should I put it? It's basically like an <color=#dbc291ff>orienteering race for creations</color>.

t_ch: 形态各异的小家伙需要越过重重障碍，抵达赛道终点，争夺更高的名次…听起来是不是还挺有意思的？
t_en: These unique little ones need to overcome various obstacles to reach the finish line and compete for the top spots... Sounds pretty fun, doesn't it?

t_ch: 好像在电视上看过这种节目…
t_en: I think I've seen this kind of show on TV...

t_ch: 空间站缺冰箱了是吧？
t_en: Are the rewards from this race any good?

t_ch: 更重要的是——先说清楚，这是科员们想出来的点子——为了给参与者增添更多趣味性，赛程中还特别增加了<color=#dbc291ff>应援环节</color>。
t_en: More importantly — and I want to make it clear that this was our researchers' idea — to make things more exciting for the participants, the race will feature a special <color=#dbc291ff>cheering segment</color>.

t_ch: 可以为看好的小家伙应援，要是它赢得比赛的话，你就会获得极其丰厚的奖励！
t_en: You can cheer for a promising little one, and if it wins the competition, you'll receive incredibly generous rewards!

t_ch: 请为我应援喵！为我应援谢谢喵！
t_en: Cheer for me, meow! Thanks for cheering for me, meow!

t_ch: 猜错了…会有惩罚吗？
t_en: And if I pick the wrong one... is there a penalty?

t_ch: 当然不会啦。都说是个游戏，你是来帮忙的，怎么能让你破费呢？
t_en: Of course not! This is just a game, and you're here to help out. How could I let you pay for anything?

t_ch: 安安心心地坐在观众席，看那群小家伙的精彩表现就好。
t_en: Just sit back in the stands, and enjoy the little ones putting on a great show.

t_ch: 我已经完全明白了！
t_en: I totally get it now!

t_ch: 噢，还有件事，你肯定会感兴趣的。
t_en: Oh, and there's one more thing you'll definitely be interested in.

t_ch: 这场造物们的越野训练得到了星际和平公司的资金支持，因此，叽米先生会作为解说出场，实时播报赛况哦。
t_en: This creations' great obstacle race is sponsored by the IPC, so Mr. Owlbert will be providing live commentary as our commentator.

t_ch: 怎么样，就说不会无聊的吧？这些糕点先给你，待会应援的时候用得上。
t_en: See? I told you it would be fun. Here, take these pastries. You'll need them for the cheering later.

t_ch: 准备好了，我们现在就出发喵！
t_en: We're all set, let's head out now, meow!

t_ch: 呼…我闭目一想，便知道你会在这个时间来找我。伙伴们都说我料事如神、算无遗策，断然不是空口无凭、空穴来风哪。
t_en: Hmm... I knew you'd come find me at this time. They say I'm prophetic and never miss a beat. Trust me, that's not just empty talk or baseless rumors.

t_ch: 承蒙大家厚爱，推举我为本次赛跑大会的主持者，也欢迎各位嘉宾前来观赛咪。
t_en: Thank you to everyone who chose me as the host of this racing event, I am humbled by your support. Welcome, honored guests, to the spectacle, meow!

t_ch: 点心糕饼，向前冲——
t_en: Time to make a dash for snacks!

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 嗯。本次赛事还会持续很长时间，挑方便的时候来玩就好咪。
t_en: Alrighty. The event will be running for quite a while, so just drop by whenever it's convenient for you!

t_ch: 小家伙们跌跌撞撞冲向终点的样子，真是有趣呢。
t_en: It's quite amusing to watch those little ones stumbling their way to the finish line.

t_ch: 规则…应该都明白了？后续就麻烦你啦，在空间站里开开心心地陪造物们玩吧。
t_en: Got the rules... all clear? I'll leave it to you then. Have fun playing with the creations on the space station.

t_ch: 这是正经的训练项目吗？
t_en: Is this really a proper training program?

t_ch: 看起来不太像，对吧？其实刚接到这个任务的时候，我也挺疑惑的。
t_en: Doesn't quite look like it, right? To be honest, I was pretty confused too when I first got this mission.

t_ch: 「障碍」、「封闭系统」、「干涉与观测」…记得最初的方案上写着这几个关键词，但阮•梅女士也没有向我们透露太多就是。
t_en: "Obstacles," "closed system," "interference and observation"... I remember these keywords were written in the initial proposal, but Miss Ruan Mei didn't reveal much more to us than that.

t_ch: 天才们的想法，或许就是这么难懂吧。不过，够有趣就行啦。
t_en: Maybe geniuses' ideas are just that hard to grasp. But hey, as long as it's fun, that's what matters!

t_ch: 糕点用完了该怎么办呢？
t_en: What if I run out of pastries?

t_ch: 喏，我会帮你设置好小机器人的程序，需要糕点的时候，找它就行了。
t_en: Here, I'll program the Little Robot for you. Just ask it whenever you need pastries.

t_ch: 为了保障公平性，只有当糕点不够的时候才能去取哦，所以还是多想想办法吧。
t_en: But to ensure fairness, you can only get more when you have insufficient pastries. So, you might want to strategize a little.

t_ch: 包在我身上。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我就知道，你肯定和小家伙们很合得来。费用的事就交给我，尽管去尝试吧。
t_en: I knew you'd get along well with the little ones. Don't worry about the costs, I'll handle that. Just go ahead and give it a try.

t_ch: 那么，祝你玩得开心！
t_en: Well then, have fun!

t_ch: 嘶嘶。别担心，糕点用完，是常有的事情。嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. Don't worry, pastries run out all the time. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。跟我一起，念出那句咒语，很快就能东山再起。嘶嘶——
t_en: Zztzzt. Say the incantation with me, and you can make a comeback. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 「请给我更多糕点吧！」
t_en: "I beg you, give me more pastries!"

t_ch: 请给我更多糕点吧！
t_en: I beg you, give me more pastries!

t_ch: 这也是艾丝妲布置的任务吗？
t_en: Is this another mission from Asta?

t_ch: 不是。嘶嘶。是为你预留的后门，无需花费，直接获取点心。嘶嘶。
t_en: No. Zztzzt. It's a backdoor reserved just for you, get free snacks, no questions asked. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 喊出来：「请给我更多糕点吧！」嘶嘶，我会为你服务——
t_en: Shout this: "I beg you, give me more pastries!" Zztzzt. I'll be at your service.

t_ch: 请给我更多糕点吧！
t_en: I beg you, give me more pastries!

t_ch: 嘶嘶。糕点出炉。拿去吧，回到属于你的战场，夺回属于你的东西吧。嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. Fresh from the oven. Take them and return to your battlefield. Reclaim what's rightfully yours. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。每个人都值得第二次机会。
t_en: Zztzzt. Everyone deserves a second chance.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。真巧，我们第二次见面了。难道，你这么快就把糕点又用完了？嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. Look who's here... we meet again. Don't tell me you've already run out of pastries, again? Zztzzt.

t_ch: 借用空间站最近很流行的台词：「你好会玩游戏呀~」
t_en: To quote a popular saying on the space station: "You're such a pro gamer~"

t_ch: 请给我更多糕点吧！
t_en: I beg you, give me more pastries!

t_ch: 少说怪话，赶快把糕点给我。
t_en: Cut the snark and hand over those pastries.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。不对。你想要，就得自己来拿。嘶嘶，按约定好的方式——
t_en: Zztzzt. No, that's not right. If you want it, come and take it. But of course, by our agreed terms...

t_ch: 请给我更多糕点吧！
t_en: I beg you, give me more pastries!

t_ch: 嘶嘶。给你给你。回去吧，在哪里跌倒的，就在哪里站起来。嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. Here you go, take them. What doesn't kill you only makes you stronger. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。每个人都值得第三次机会。
t_en: Zztzzt. Everyone deserves a third chance.

t_ch: 嘶嘶。怎么还是你，我已经不知道该说什么好了。是直接请求还是走程序？嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. Why are you here again? I don't even know what to say anymore. Do you want to beg right away, or go through the standard procedure? Zztzzt.

t_ch: 我的忍耐是有限度的！
t_en: My patience has its limits!

t_ch: 呵，跟我的球棒去说吧。
t_en: Heh, tell that to my bat.

t_ch: 据说，修理电视机最好的方式是用力敲几下，想必同样的办法对修理机器人也会奏效吧？
t_en: They say the best way to fix a TV is to give it a few good whacks. I bet the same trick would work on robots, too, right?

t_ch: 嘶嘶。我很抱歉。对不起。你是对的。糕点…糕点全给你。嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. I'm sorry. I apologize. You're right. The pastries... they are all yours now. Zztzzt...

t_ch: 嘶嘶。这是为您预留的糕点，请收下。请妥善保管。嘶嘶。
t_en: Zztzzt. These pastries are reserved for you. Please accept them, and make sure to take good care of them. Zztzzt.

t_ch: 点心机器人忠实履行着自己的职责，把更多糕点递给了你。
t_en: The pastry robot dutifully fulfills its task, offering you more pastries.

t_ch: 好吧，尽管顺利拿到了重返赛场的资格，但你不禁感到了一丝无趣。
t_en: Well, even though you successfully got your qualification to return to the arena, you can't help but feel a bit bored.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你，阮•梅女士在我这里留下了个大大的宝箱。
t_en: Just between us, Miss Ruan Mei left a huge chest in my care.

t_ch: 想要吗？但现在还不能给你。
t_en: Want it? But I can't give it to you yet.

t_ch: 阮•梅女士留下的藏宝箱，投进去的是糕点，吐出来的是好多好多奇怪的东西。
t_en: If you feed pastries to the chest Miss Ruan Mei left, out comes all sorts of weird stuff!

t_ch: 想要吗？想要的话，就拿大家都喜欢的糕点来换吧。
t_en: Want it? If you do, trade me some of those pastries everyone loves.

t_ch: 都有些什么好东西？
t_en: So, what goodies do you have?

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 好哦，什么时候来都可以。我会一直在这呆着的。
t_en: Sure thing, come whenever you like. I'll be hanging around here anyway.

t_ch: 承蒙大家厚爱，推举我为本次赛跑大会的主持者，也欢迎各位嘉宾前来观赛咪。
t_en: Thank you to everyone who chose me as the host of this racing event, I am humbled by your support. Welcome, honored guests, to the spectacle, meow!

t_ch: 下一场比赛即将开始，你要来参与应援吗？
t_en: The next match is about to begin. Care to join in the cheering?

t_ch: 点心糕饼，向前冲——
t_en: Time to make a dash for snacks!

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 嗯。本次赛事还会持续很长时间，挑方便的时候来玩就好咪。
t_en: Alrighty. The event will be running for quite a while, so just drop by whenever it's convenient for you!

t_ch: 比赛越是激烈，就越要争取应援者的支持。这位朋友！我们来做个交易如何？
t_en: The fiercer the race, the more we need our supporters' backing. Hey friend! How about we make a deal?

t_ch: 作为回报，这些亮晶晶的东西送给你，你肯定会喜欢的喵。
t_en: In return, I'll give you these shiny things. You'll love them, meow!

t_ch: 先给我看看！
t_en: Let me see it first!

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 没关系。我的耐心很充足，想合作的时候，随时来找我就行了喵。
t_en: No worries. I've got plenty of patience. Just come find me whenever you're ready to cooperate, meow.

t_ch: 喵！我最喜欢和大家一起玩了，热热闹闹的，最后「砰」的一下——多有趣呀！
t_en: Meow! I love playing with everyone! It's so lively and fun, and then BOOM! Isn't that just awesome!?

t_ch: 是吧是吧？你肯定也这么想吧？要珍惜和朋友共同度过的时光喵！
t_en: Right, right? You think so too, don't you? Gotta cherish the time spent with friends, meow!

t_ch: 我也同意！
t_en: Yeah, I agree!

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 真没意思。记得要多来找我玩喵。
t_en: Boring. Make sure you come hang out with me more often, meow.

t_ch: 知己知彼，才能战无不胜。所有造物的情报，都在我的掌握之中喵。
t_en: Know thyself, know thy enemy. A thousand battles, a thousand victories. The intel of all creations is at my fingertips, meow.

t_ch: 你想听听吗？不收费的。
t_en: Wanna hear about it? No charge.

t_ch: 让我听听！
t_en: I'm all ears!

t_ch: 待会再来找你们玩吧。
t_en: I'll come hang out with you later.

t_ch: 让我算算，下次见是什么时候喵……
t_en: Let me see... when will we meet again, meow...

t_ch: 样式奇特的卷轴默立于此，周身散发出暗红光芒，仿佛是自这血色天幕垂落的一声哀泣。
t_en: A strangely wrought scroll stands in silence, radiating a dark red glow, like a sorrowful cry falling from the blood-colored sky.

t_ch: 卷轴对面，一名女孩的记忆定格在凝望的时刻，掩映在余晖中。
t_en: Facing the scroll, a memorialized image of a girl is frozen in a moment of gazing, shrouded in the afterglow.

t_ch: …晨昏之眼看见一切，其目光如天穹般深远。
t_en: ...The Eye of Twilight is all-seeing, its vision as far-reaching as the sky dome.

t_ch: 可如今天空愀然失色，圣城满目疮痍。就连乌拉尼娅的「眼睛」，也变得模糊不清。
t_en: But now the sky has lost its color, and the holy city lies in ruins. Even Urania's "eyes" have grown blurred and dim.

t_ch: 我正好认识几位医师朋友。
t_en: I happen to know a few doctors.

t_ch: 谢谢你，好心的{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。但乌拉尼娅的「眼睛」并非双目，而是这由飞鸟所衔的卷轴。
t_en: Thank you, kind {F#lady}{M#sir}. But Urania's "eyes" are not eyes at all, just scrolls carried by birds.

t_ch: 或许只是因为光线不足。
t_en: Maybe it's because of the dim light.

t_ch: 谢谢你的安慰，好心的{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。但乌拉尼娅的「眼睛」并非双目，而是这由飞鸟所衔的卷轴。
t_en: Thank you for your comfort, kind {F#lady}{M#sir}. But Urania's "eyes" are not eyes at all, just scrolls carried by birds.

t_ch: 它是艾格勒赐下的圣卷。乌拉尼娅要借助此物来记录天空下的所见，作为晨昏之眼目光的延伸。
t_en: They are holy scrolls bestowed by Aquila. With them, Urania records all that unfolds beneath the sky, as an extension of the Eye of Twilight's gaze.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你知道么？天黑之后，城里到处都是怪物。幸好一名卫士发现了躲在罐子里的我，将乌拉尼娅带到生命花园。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you know what? After dark, monsters are everywhere in the city. Luckily, a guard found Urania hiding in a jar and brought Urania to the Garden of Life.

t_ch: 彩虹色的屏障庇护着在此避难的大家，公民组建起守卫队伍，英雄们奔赴抗争前线。
t_en: The rainbow-colored barrier shelters all who seek refuge here. Citizens have formed defense squads, and heroes have rushed to the front line of resistance.

t_ch: 于是，乌拉尼娅就把听来的传奇，全都记录在了这卷启目集上。
t_en: So Urania recorded every legend Urania has heard on this scroll of enlightenment.

t_ch: 这一切确实值得记录。
t_en: All of this is definitely worth recording.

t_ch: 很珍贵的故事。
t_en: A precious story.

t_ch: 对吧对吧？我也这样觉得。乌拉尼娅想让「天空」看到，奥赫玛的余晖中，人们仍然团结，愿以信念与勇气来捍卫新世界的曙光。
t_en: Right, right? Urania feels the same. Urania wants the "Sky" to see that, in Okhema's afterglow, people still stand united, ready to guard the dawn of a new world with their faith and courage.

t_ch: 然而在乌拉尼娅整理故事时，却发现卷轴上的字迹变成了一片混沌。
t_en: Yet when Urania tried to put the stories in order, Urania found that the writing on the scroll had turned into chaos.

t_ch: 不知道发生了什么，但我不愿放弃这些伟大的记忆，便只好留在这里，等待传奇的讲述者们归来。
t_en: Urania doesn't know what happened, but Urania could not give up these magnificent memories. So Urania remained here, waiting for the storytellers of legend to return.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你愿意读读卷轴上的故事吗？然后告诉乌拉尼娅，它们有没有被正确地书写下来，好不好？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, will you read the stories on the scroll? Then tell Urania if they were written down correctly, please?

t_ch: 女孩的目光是如此恳切，令你几乎忘却自己面对的只是往昔幻影。或许启目集记录的除了故事本身，还有关于世界的更多信息。甚至——
t_en: The girl's gaze was so earnest that you almost forgot you were looking at nothing more than a shadow of the past. Perhaps the scroll of enlightenment held more than just stories. Perhaps it also carried truths about the world itself, and even...

t_ch: 也包括眼前女孩对她自己的记忆，以及她对史诗传述的期待。
t_en: Including the girl's memories of herself, and her longing for the telling of epic tales.

t_ch: 好吧，我答应你。
t_en: Alright, I promise you.

t_ch: 谢谢你，好心的{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！
t_en: Thank you, kind {F#lady}{M#sir}!

t_ch: 女孩露出欣慰的笑容，其记忆中的身形随之消散。奥赫玛的余晖中，唯余启目集留在原地，致以永志不忘的纪念。
t_en: The girl smiled in relief, and her form born of memory faded away. In the afterglow of Okhema, only the scroll of enlightenment remained, standing as an everlasting memorial.

t_ch: 您来了，太好了，我们的项目有救了！
t_en: You're here, what a relief! Our project might just be saved!

t_ch: 可算把您盼来了！
t_en: We've been expecting you!

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 是这样的，空间站有一套自研的模拟战斗工具，目前是我俩在接手——
t_en: So, here's the thing: the space station has a self-developed combat simulation tool, and right now, it's just the two of us handling it —

t_ch: 哪里哪里，是布凯辛前辈在跟进，我在旁边学习，顺便给前辈打下手这样。
t_en: Oh, not really, Grumpy is taking the lead. I'm just learning on the side and helping out where I can.

t_ch: 咳咳，在{NICKNAME}前辈面前就别说这种话了，咱们加起来都不如{NICKNAME}一根手指头。
t_en: Ahem, save the speech in front of {NICKNAME}. The two of us combined don't even measure up to an ounce of {NICKNAME}'s ability.

t_ch: 那肯定呀，{NICKNAME}可是单手接末日兽的大神啊。
t_en: Agreed. {NICKNAME} is the legend who single-handedly took down the Doomsday Beast!

t_ch: 而且{NICKNAME}前辈也没少帮咱们空间站的忙，说起来还真是要感谢您。
t_en: Plus, {NICKNAME} has helped our space station a lot, so we really owe you a big thanks.

t_ch: 得益于前几次的数据和热心人士的鼎力相助，我们的研究取得了相当不错的成果，艾丝妲站长说黑塔女士可是在我们的数据面前停留了足足<unbreak>0.3</unbreak>秒！
t_en: Thanks to the data from previous trials and the help of an awesome person, our research has made great progress. Lead Researcher Asta said Madam Herta stayed for a whole <unbreak>0.3</unbreak> seconds before our data!

t_ch: 「热心人士」就是我！
t_en: Talking about some "awesome person"? That would be me!

t_ch: 对呀，所以我把第二作者让给了您，要是没有您，我们的项目指定要凉了……
t_en: Right, so I gave you the second author spot. Without you, our project would've been doomed...

t_ch: …那不就是扫了一眼吗？
t_en: ...It was just a quick glance, wasn't it?

t_ch: 对我们这种普通科员来说，做出来的东西能让黑塔女士入眼就是莫大的成功了。
t_en: For regular researchers like us, simply getting something that catches Madam Herta's eye is worth bragging about.

t_ch: 艾丝妲站长说，她认为我们这个项目还有发展的空间，因此从黑塔女士那边申请到了一些重量级的实验数据，希望我们能在项目里模拟出来。
t_en: Lead Researcher Asta said she thinks this project still has room to grow, so she got some top-tier experimental data from Madam Herta, hoping we can simulate it in our project.

t_ch: 不过嘛…我们比对过这批数据，在空间站没有任何科员能匹配成功。后来还是艾丝妲站长提醒了我们——
t_en: But, we've cross-checked this data, and no one at the space station could match it. Then, Lead Researcher Asta pointed something out to us —

t_ch: 这批数据，和您的匹配度为<unbreak>100%</unbreak>，看着就像是…为您量身定做的。
t_en: This batch of data is a perfect <unbreak>100%</unbreak> match for you. It looks like... it was made just for you.

t_ch: 一定是黑塔干的。
t_en: It's gotta be Herta's work.

t_ch: …所以你们就来找我了？
t_en: ...So that's why you came to me?

t_ch: 黑塔女士的确希望您来当测试员。
t_en: Madam Herta really wants you to be the tester.

t_ch: 不过说实话我们也不太清楚这里面究竟是什么…只是按照您的战斗风格与习惯，进行了针对性的优化与微调。
t_en: Honestly, we're not entirely sure what's inside... we just optimized and tweaked it based on your combat style and routines.

t_ch: 站长只说这批数据是黑塔女士所拥有的，保密等级极高，我们此前从未接触过，所以我们也不太确定里面装载的模拟数据究竟属于哪个文明世界。
t_en: The Lead Researcher only mentioned that this batch of data belongs to Madam Herta and is highly confidential, something we've never had access to before. So, we're not entirely sure which civilization's data is actually contained within.

t_ch: 而且数据量巨大，我们花了好久才完成整合。
t_en: Plus, the data is huge, it took us forever to integrate it.

t_ch: 但它既然是黑塔女士的珍藏，又有{NICKNAME}当特邀测试员，我想只要我们肯花功夫，这研究出来的成果肯定非同凡响。
t_en: Since it's Madam Herta's collection, and we've got {NICKNAME} as the special guest tester, I'm sure that if we put in the work, we'll get solid results.

t_ch: 所以这次的版本我们叫它「星芒启明」，希望黑塔女士的智慧能启迪空间站的大伙儿。
t_en: So, we've named this version "Morning Starlight," hoping Madam Herta's wisdom will inspire everyone at the space station.

t_ch: 数据都已经装载到终端里了，要试试看吗?
t_en: The data's all loaded into the terminal. Wanna give it a try?

t_ch: 能多点给奖励吗？
t_en: Any chance we could get more rewards?

t_ch: 放心吧，艾丝妲站长可是专门拨了一笔经费下来的，我们绝不敢贪墨分毫！
t_en: Don't worry, Lead Researcher Asta specifically set aside some funds for this. We wouldn't dare take a penny!

t_ch: 但愿不会太棘手。
t_en: Hope it won't be too tricky.

t_ch: 有您帮忙的话，肯定不会有问题的！
t_en: With you helping, it won't be a problem!

t_ch: 太好啦！我就知道您会答应的！
t_en: Awesome! I knew you'd say yes!

t_ch: 请您放心，我们会全程监控系统运行的。如果有任何异常，会立即中止测试。那么…测试的事就拜托您了！
t_en: Don't worry, we'll be monitoring the system the whole time. If anything goes wrong, we'll stop the test right away. So... we're counting on you for the test!

t_ch: …前辈，别看数据啦，贵客前来可不能失了礼数啊！
t_en: ...Grumpy, stop looking at the data, a guest of honor just arrived!

t_ch: 啊？！是{NICKNAME}大神？等等，我看看，咦已经打通了？！
t_en: Huh!? It's {NICKNAME}? Hold on, let me check... huh, it's connected already!?

t_ch: 不愧是您！话说，里面究竟是什么东西啊？
t_en: That was quick! Any idea what's inside?

t_ch: 平平无奇的怪物和熟人。
t_en: Just some ordinary monsters and familiar faces.

t_ch: …大概是我记忆里的人吧。
t_en: ...Probably people from my memory.

t_ch: 原来如此，从数据量来看，这么庞大的内容果然是只有您这样的无名客才能拥有的。
t_en: I see. With this amount of data, only someone like you, a Nameless, could have something this massive.

t_ch: 就是，像我们普通人，终其一生的回忆整合到一起，大概也就是现在的百分之一吧。
t_en: Exactly, for ordinary people like us, even if we combined all our memories from our lives, it'd probably only be about 1% of this.

t_ch: 对了，您觉得难度如何，是否有需要调整的地方？
t_en: By the way, how do you find the difficulty? Anything that needs adjusting?

t_ch: 小菜一碟，太简单了。
t_en: Not really. It's just a piece of cake.

t_ch: 挺难的，还好我技高一筹。
t_en: It's tough, but luckily, I'm a pro.

t_ch: 我就知道，不愧是您。
t_en: Figures. That is so you.

t_ch: 真的很感谢您，我们和艾丝妲站长一起准备了些小礼物，虽然不是什么珍贵的东西，但是希望您能收下。
t_en: We're really grateful. Lead Researcher Asta and I prepared some small gifts. Nothing fancy, but we hope you'll take them.

t_ch: 要不然…您帮了这么大忙，我们真不知道该如何是好了。
t_en: Otherwise... we really don't know how to repay your huge help.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，请您一定要收下！
t_en: Please take the gifts. We insist.

t_ch: 测试的事…我看目前的数据还未完全验收。是过程中遇到什么麻烦了吗？请您放心，我们会根据您的身体数据实时调整的。
t_en: About the test... It seems the data hasn't been fully verified yet. Did you run into any issues? Don't worry, we'll adjust it based on your body data in real time.

t_ch: 绝对不会有危险的，有任何异常，我第一时间拔线！
t_en: No danger at all, if anything goes wrong, I'll pull the plug right away!

t_ch: 那么…测试的事就拜托您了！
t_en: Well then... we're counting on you for the test!

t_ch: 列车上不知什么时候出现了…一块棋盘？
t_en: When did this... chunk of chessboard appear on the Express?

t_ch: 虽然用「块」来形容棋盘很奇怪，但确实没有更贴切的词了。你伸出手，试着触碰它……
t_en: Though it's odd to describe the chessboard as a "chunk," there really isn't a more fitting word. You reach out your hand, trying to touch it...

t_ch: 「均衡…」一个低沉的声音从中传出，「宇宙应当，均衡…」
t_en: "The Equilibrium..." a deep voice echoed from within, "The universe must be... in equilibrium..."

t_ch: 又来？
t_en: Again?

t_ch: 列车真是越来越热闹了…
t_en: The Express is getting livelier than ever...

t_ch: 你也是忆者？
t_en: You're a Memokeeper too?

t_ch: 「均衡…」那个声音重复道。
t_en: "The Equilibrium..." the voice repeated.

t_ch: 你的存在打破了零和，你已经攫取太多{RUBY_B#虚数能}能量{RUBY_E#}。均衡，必须维系。
t_en: Your existence disrupts the zero-sum. You've seized too much {RUBY_B#Imaginary Energy}energy{RUBY_E#}. The Equilibrium must be maintained.

t_ch: 你要被放置在天平上称量，你需要<color=#dbc291ff>一场审判</color>。
t_en: You will be weighed on the scales. And you shall be part of <color=#dbc291ff>a judgment</color>.

t_ch: 与「截然相反」的力量对弈。你将接受审判，亦将做出审判；你既会是棋子，也会是执棋之人。
t_en: A game of countering opposing forces. You will be judged, and you shall judge. You will be both a pawn and the player.

t_ch: 你到底是什么东西？
t_en: What the heck are you?

t_ch: 「均衡」的仲裁官，仲裁官中的「结绳人」。
t_en: The Arbitrator of Equilibrium, and the "Knot-Tier" among Arbitrators.

t_ch: 衡量、判断、修正…我们追随{RUBY_B#「均衡」互}祂{RUBY_E#}的脚步，惟愿寰宇归于平衡。
t_en: Measure, judge, rectify... We follow in {RUBY_B#HooH the Equilibrium}THEIR{RUBY_E#} footsteps, seeking only to bring balance to the universe.

t_ch: 我们是二元概念的具象，从不轻易示现。但你不同，那条未被你踏足的小径，太过刺眼……
t_en: We are the embodiment of duality, rarely revealing ourselves. But you're different. That path you haven't taken yet is far too glaring...

t_ch: 告诉我更多仲裁官的事。
t_en: Tell me more about the Arbitrator.

t_ch: 万物皆有联系，那些不能看清它的人被称为{RUBY_B#凡人}沉睡者{RUBY_E#}，而仲裁官可以干涉联系，让一切归于均衡。
t_en: All things are connected. Those who cannot see these connections are called {RUBY_B#mortals}Slumberers{RUBY_E#}, while Arbitrators can intervene in these connections to restore the Equilibrium.

t_ch: 我们与{RUBY_B#凡人}沉睡者{RUBY_E#}的距离最近，履行连接一切分离之物的责任，故被称作「结绳人」。
t_en: We are closest to the {RUBY_B#mortal}Slumberers{RUBY_E#}, fulfilling our duty to connect all that is separated, hence we are called "the Knot-tiers."

t_ch: 我知道这些就够了。
t_en: That's all I need to know.

t_ch: 为什么我要接受审判？
t_en: Why should I be put on trial?

t_ch: 均衡，必须维系——
t_en: The Equilibrium must be maintained—

t_ch: 我等仲裁官着眼于宏大的均衡：无论自然生长的植物，还是人为修建的塔楼，在我们眼里都无区别。唯有天平两端值得关注。
t_en: We Arbitrators focus on the grand Equilibrium: Whether it's naturally growing plants or artificially built towers, we see no difference. Only the two ends of the scale are worth our attention.

t_ch: 而今，你已站在更重的那一端，我们需往另一端添上砝码。
t_en: Now that you stand on the heavier end, we need to add weights to the other side.

t_ch: 对弈是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean by "countering"?

t_ch: 世界万物皆有反相，正如磁体有两极、电荷有正负、动物有生死……
t_en: Everything in the world has its opposite, just like magnets have poles, charges have positive and negative, and living beings have life and death...

t_ch: 你的对手，便是你的反相。你若是正，它便是邪；你若是恶，它便是善。
t_en: Your opponent is your inverse. If you are righteous, they are wicked. Contrarily, if you are evil, they are good.

t_ch: 仲裁官会搭建一座桥梁，令你们在虚拟的搏斗中交换{RUBY_B#虚数能}能量{RUBY_E#}，就像沙漏的两端，最终达成和谐一统。
t_en: The Arbitrator will build a bridge, allowing you to exchange {RUBY_B#Imaginary Energy}energy{RUBY_E#} in virtual combat, and like the two ends of an hourglass, ultimately achieving harmonious unity.

t_ch: 此即零和——对弈结束后，什么都不会增多，什么也不会减少。
t_en: This is zero-sum — After the countering ends, nothing increases, nor decreases.

t_ch: 那就来吧。
t_en: Then let's do this!

t_ch: 记住，无论胜败，零和都将达成。
t_en: Remember, win or lose, zero-sum will be achieved.

t_ch: 得益于你的配合，世界离均衡又近了一步。
t_en: Thanks to your cooperation, the world has taken another step closer to the Equilibrium.

t_ch: 敌人怎么有点眼熟？
t_en: Why does this enemy look kinda familiar?

t_ch: 它们是我们捏塑的虚影。如果你期待一场真实的博弈，大可看向旅途前方。
t_en: They are mere shadows we mold. If you're looking for a real game of countering, just look ahead on your journey.

t_ch: 好难啊！
t_en: This is so hard!

t_ch: 零和的关键不在胜负。无论你取得何种结果，都能促使天平归位。
t_en: The key to zero-sum isn't about winning or losing. Whatever outcome you achieve, it will always help restore balance to the scales.

t_ch: 我有奖励吗？
t_en: Do I get a reward?

t_ch: 你想得到怎样的奖赏呢？
t_en: What kind of reward would you like?

t_ch: 强大的力量。
t_en: Immense power.

t_ch: 失落的秘宝。
t_en: Lost treasure.

t_ch: 财富与地位。
t_en: Wealth and status.

t_ch: 可靠的伙伴。
t_en: A reliable partner.

t_ch: 星琼与车票。
t_en: Stellar Jades and Boarding Passes.

t_ch: 有趣……
t_en: How interesting...

t_ch: 我无权赐予你任何礼物。但只要对弈继续，定会有珍贵之物从中示现。
t_en: I have no right to bestow any gifts upon you. But as long as the countering continues, precious things will surely reveal themselves.

t_ch: 无名之人、无命之人，终有一日，你会意识到自己的本质。届时，你将面临真正的取舍……
t_en: Nameless one, fateless one, one day you shall realize your true nature. And when that time comes, you'll be faced with a real choice...

t_ch: 「均衡…」一个低沉的声音从中传出，「多亏你的配合，世界离均衡又近了一步。」
t_en: "The Equilibrium..." a deep voice echoed, "Thanks to your cooperation, the world has taken another step closer to Equilibrium."

t_ch: 赐我「均衡」的审判吧。
t_en: Grant me the Judgment of the Equilibrium.

t_ch: 进入「星尘之礼」
t_en: Enter "Gift of Stardust"

t_ch: 可以分享你的一些见闻吗？
t_en: Can you share some of your experiences with me?

t_ch: 嗯…我有许多故事可以讲述。但它们与「均衡」的关联，需要你自行参悟。
t_en: Hmm... I have many stories to tell. But their connection to the Equilibrium is something you'll need to figure out for yourself.

t_ch: 雨季是凯尔萨斯星最大的威胁。当三折河的干流决堤时，一位巨人用身躯堵住了裂口，为民众争取宝贵的逃命时间。
t_en: The rainy season is Kalsas's greatest threat. When the main stream of the Three-fold River burst its banks, a giant used its body to block the fissure, buying precious time for people to escape.

t_ch: 数周后，难民们重返故地，发现巨人的身体已经化作一块石碑。此后的数个琥珀纪，三折河再未出现过洪水。
t_en: Weeks later, when the refugees returned to their homeland, they found the giant's body had turned into a stone monument. For many Amber Eras after that, the Three-fold River never flooded again.

t_ch: 这位巨人被后世尊称为「川伯」，它所化的石碑则是「川伯的丰碑」。念及其恩泽的人们纷纷前来祭奠，久而久之，那堤坝竟成了一方圣地。
t_en: The giant was later revered as "River Elder," and the stone monument it turned into became known as "River Elder's Monument." People who remembered its benevolence came to pay their respects, and over time, the dam became a sacred site.

t_ch: 再后来，凯尔萨斯人建立了庞大的帝国，筑起了高楼大厦，文明水平一跃而起。
t_en: Later, the Kalsasians built a vast empire and erected towering buildings, with which their civilization experienced a massive advancement.

t_ch: 当地总督不满于这种先祖崇拜，便命人翻新河口周围的堤坝，随后将石碑整个拆除。
t_en: The local governor, displeased with this ancestral worship, ordered the renovation of the levees around the river mouth and had the stone monument completely demolished.

t_ch: 于是三折河爆发了有史以来最大的水灾，冲毁了堤坝和沿岸城市。即使有星际和平公司协助，重建工作依旧花费了数年时间。
t_en: And so, the Three-fold River experienced its worst flood in history, destroying dams and cities along its banks. Even with the Interastral Peace Corporation's assistance, the reconstruction took several years.

t_ch: 嗯…我有许多故事可以讲述。但它们与「均衡」的关联，需要你自行参悟。
t_en: Hmm... I have many stories to tell. But their connection to the Equilibrium is something you'll need to figure out for yourself.

t_ch: 这是一个王子复仇的故事。墨洛温王国历史上有过许多任统治者，泽勒家族便是其中之一。
t_en: This is a story of a prince's revenge. Throughout the history of Morrowen Kingdom, there have been many rulers, and the Zeller Family was one of them.

t_ch: 当起义军冲进泽勒王的寝宫时，年仅9岁的小王子看清了领头者的面容——国王最信赖、也最贤能的大臣。
t_en: When the rebels stormed into King Zeller's bedchamber, the 9-year-old prince clearly saw the face of their leader, the most trusted and capable Minister, whom the king had relied on above all others.

t_ch: 王子花了十年的时间筹备这场复仇。在此期间，曾经的贤臣已经被百姓推上王座、开启了一个崭新的时代。
t_en: The prince spent ten years preparing for his revenge. During this time, the former wise Minister had been crowned king by the people, ushering in a new era.

t_ch: 在一场盛大的战斗后，王子终于把尖刀刺进了大臣的胸膛。他向百姓指认弑君者的罪行，却被石子与怒骂声包围。
t_en: After an epic battle, the prince finally plunged his dagger into the Minister's chest. When he revealed the regicide to his people, he was only to be met with a barrage of stones and angry curses.

t_ch: 人们在大臣的棺椁前哭泣，称王子为「可耻的刺客」、「暴君的余孤」、「断送王国未来的灾星」。
t_en: People wept before the Minister's coffin, calling the prince "the shameful assassin," "orphan of the tyrant," and "the ill-starred harbinger who would doom the kingdom's future."

t_ch: 当处刑的铡刀落下时，王子才终于接受了事实：他父亲是无能的暴君，大臣才是治世能才。人们为他父亲的死鼓掌，而叹惋于大臣死得太早……
t_en: When the execution blade fell, the prince finally accepted the truth: His father was an incompetent tyrant, while the Minister was the true talent in governing. People applauded his father's death, yet lamented that the Minister had died too soon...

t_ch: 嗯…我有许多故事可以讲述。但它们与「均衡」的关联，需要你自行参悟。
t_en: Hmm... I have many stories to tell. But their connection to the Equilibrium is something you'll need to figure out for yourself.

t_ch: 拉蒂卡兰原本是一颗冻土星球。一只离群的因菲诺巨龙来到此处，决心将其改造成宜居的熔岩地貌。
t_en: Ladiklan was once a frozen planet. Then one day, a lone Infernalian Dragon arrived here, determined to transform it into a habitable lava landscape.

t_ch: 它以口中炎息融化冰川，露出坚实的土地，随后便因力竭沉睡了数个琥珀纪。
t_en: With its fiery breath, the Giant Dragon melted the glaciers to reveal solid ground beneath, and then fell into a deep slumber for several Amber Eras, exhausted from the effort.

t_ch: 在此期间，一伙无名客抵达了拉蒂卡兰，利用巨龙留下的水资源开垦土地、建立聚落。当巨龙醒来时，这颗星球已经变得鸟语花香。
t_en: During this period, a group of Nameless arrived at Ladiklan, using the water resources left by the Giant Dragon to cultivate the land and establish settlements. When the Dragon awoke, the planet had already become a paradise teeming with birds and flowers.

t_ch: 巨龙愤怒地发出质问，为何要霸占它的星球；无名客的后裔却坚称，这里的一切都是由祖辈亲手建立的。
t_en: The Dragon furiously demanded to know why they had seized its planet, but the descendants of Nameless insisted that everything here had been built by their ancestors' own hands.

t_ch: 战争就此打响。最终，拉蒂卡兰人消灭了那只巨龙。可当硝烟散尽时，绿草如茵的大地已经化作焦土，再也长不出庄稼。
t_en: And so the war began. In the end, the Ladiklan people slayed the Giant Dragon. But when the smoke cleared, the once lush green land had turned into scorched earth where no crops would ever grow again.

t_ch: 资源危机让拉蒂卡兰人重新拿起武器，陷入了漫长的混战。现在，那里只剩下一片废墟。
t_en: The resource crisis drove the Ladiklans to take up arms again, plunging them into a prolonged civil war. Now, all that remains are nothing but ruins.

t_ch: 有些问题想问你…
t_en: I've got a few questions I'd like to ask you...

t_ch: 你到底是什么东西？
t_en: What the heck are you?

t_ch: 「均衡」的仲裁官，仲裁官中的「结绳人」——既是头衔，亦是权柄。
t_en: The Arbitrator of Equilibrium, the "Knot-Tier" among Arbitrators: both a title and authority.

t_ch: 衡量、判断、修正…我们追随{RUBY_B#「均衡」互}祂{RUBY_E#}的脚步，惟愿寰宇归于平衡。
t_en: Measure, judge, rectify... We follow in {RUBY_B#HooH the Equilibrium}THEIR{RUBY_E#} footsteps, seeking only to bring balance to the universe.

t_ch: 我们是二元概念的具象，从不轻易示现。但你不同，那条未被你踏足的小径，太过刺眼……
t_en: We are the embodiment of duality, rarely revealing ourselves. But you're different. That path you haven't taken yet is far too glaring...

t_ch: 告诉我更多关于仲裁官的事。
t_en: Tell me more about the Arbitrator.

t_ch: 万物皆有联系，那些不能看清它的人被称为{RUBY_B#凡人}沉睡者{RUBY_E#}，而仲裁官可以干涉联系，让一切归于均衡。
t_en: All things are connected. Those who cannot see these connections are called {RUBY_B#mortals}Slumberers{RUBY_E#}, while Arbitrators can intervene in these connections to restore the Equilibrium.

t_ch: 我们与{RUBY_B#凡人}沉睡者{RUBY_E#}的距离最近，履行连接一切分离之物的责任，故被称作「结绳人」。
t_en: We are closest to the {RUBY_B#mortal}Slumberers{RUBY_E#}, fulfilling our duty to connect all that is separated, hence we are called "the Knot-tiers."

t_ch: 为什么我要接受审判？
t_en: Why should I be put on trial?

t_ch: 均衡，必须维系——
t_en: The Equilibrium must be maintained—

t_ch: 我等仲裁官着眼于宏大的均衡：无论自然生长的植物，还是人为修建的塔楼，在我们眼里都无区别。唯有天平两端值得关注。
t_en: We Arbitrators focus on the grand Equilibrium: Whether it's naturally growing plants or artificially built towers, we see no difference. Only the two ends of the scale are worth our attention.

t_ch: 而今，你已站在更重的那一端，我们需往另一端添上砝码。
t_en: Now that you stand on the heavier end, we need to add weights to the other side.

t_ch: 无名之人、无命之人，终有一日，你会意识到自己的本质。届时，你将面临真正的取舍……
t_en: Nameless one, fateless one, one day you shall realize your true nature. And when that time comes, you'll be faced with a real choice...

t_ch: 我到底在和谁对弈？
t_en: Who exactly am I countering against?

t_ch: 世界万物皆有反相，正如磁体有两极、电荷有正负、动物有生死……
t_en: Everything in the world has its opposite, just like magnets have poles, charges have positive and negative, and living beings have life and death...

t_ch: 你的对手，便是你的反相。你若是正，它便是邪；你若是恶，它便是善。
t_en: Your opponent is your inverse. If you are righteous, they are wicked. Contrarily, if you are evil, they are good.

t_ch: 仲裁官会搭建一座桥梁，令你们在虚拟的搏斗中交换{RUBY_B#虚数能}能量{RUBY_E#}，就像沙漏的两端，最终达成和谐一统。
t_en: The Arbitrator will build a bridge, allowing you to exchange {RUBY_B#Imaginary Energy}energy{RUBY_E#} in virtual combat, and like the two ends of an hourglass, ultimately achieving harmonious unity.

t_ch: 此即零和——对弈结束后，什么都不会增多，什么也不会减少。
t_en: This is zero-sum — After the countering ends, nothing increases, nor decreases.

t_ch: 没有更多问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 今天的安排是……
t_en: Today's plan is...

t_ch: 开始装修。
t_en: Start decorating.

t_ch: 开始值日。
t_en: Commence duty.

t_ch: 稍后再来。
t_en: Come back later.

t_ch: 不管看多少次，都觉得好漂亮呢……
t_en: No matter how many times I look, it's still so beautiful...

t_ch: 呀，{NICKNAME}，见到你真高兴。快来看，要见识下哀丽秘榭的宝物吗？
t_en: Ah, {NICKNAME}, it's good to see you! Come take a look. Want to see the treasure of Aedes Elysiae?

t_ch: 宝物？
t_en: Treasure?

t_ch: （上前看看）
t_en: (Step forward to look)

t_ch: 这边这边，仔细瞧——
t_en: Right here. Look closely!

t_ch: 是不是特别漂亮？
t_en: Isn't it just beautiful?

t_ch: 怎么不说话，是被自己迷倒了吗？
t_en: Cat got your tongue? Or are you just dazzled by your own reflection?

t_ch: 这不是我吗？
t_en: Isn't that just me?

t_ch: 是呀，这就是我说的，独一无二的至宝。
t_en: Why, of course. You're a one-of-a-kind treasure!

t_ch: 所以，你刚才是在…
t_en: So, you were...

t_ch: 是呀，{NICKNAME}也能理解吧？每个人都有粉扑扑的，路过镜子就走不动路的时候……
t_en: That's right, {NICKNAME}, you get it too, don't you? Sometimes, you just feel so radiant you can't help but stop in front of a mirror...

t_ch: 好啦，我没骗你。宝物就在这儿，就是这片小湖，人家的小世界。
t_en: Alright, alright. I wasn't kidding, though. The treasure is right here. This little lake. My own little world.

t_ch: 提问！{NICKNAME}喜欢独处吗？我很喜欢哦。一个人望着湖面，秋千一摇一摇，思绪就会飘到很远的地方，再回来时…自己已经睡着啦。
t_en: Can I ask? {NICKNAME}, do you like being by yourself? I do. Just staring at the lake by myself, gently swaying on the swing... My mind drifts far away, and by the time it comes back, I've already fallen asleep.

t_ch: 在这永夜之帷包裹的村庄，梦会为幻想插上翅膀。在哀丽秘榭长大的孩子，生来就是浪漫的诗人。
t_en: Here in this village shrouded by the Veil of Evernight, dreams give wings to imagination. Children who grow up in Aedes Elysiae are born poets at heart.

t_ch: 气氛难得，不如让我即兴为你讲个故事吧。
t_en: This sort of atmosphere is hard to come by... so let me share a story with you.

t_ch: 说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 好有耐心呀，我好高兴。有{NICKNAME}这样的听众在，故事才会焕发生命。
t_en: How wonderful! You're so patient, {NICKNAME}. Stories only shine when they have a listener like you.

t_ch: 万一睡着了怎么办？
t_en: What if I doze off?

t_ch: 别担心，那就把它当作睡前故事。我会用很轻、很轻的悄悄话，讲完最后一个句号哦。
t_en: Don't worry. Just think of it as a bedtime story. I'll whisper it softly and quietly until the final sentence comes to a close.

t_ch: 该从哀丽秘榭出发了吧？
t_en: Shouldn't we head out of Aedes Elysiae already?

t_ch: 收拾心绪，也是启程的一环呀。拜托啦，不会花太久的，就当是满足人家的小小心愿，可以吗？
t_en: Getting your thoughts in order is part of preparing to leave, you know? It won't take long, I promise. Just think of it as granting me a little wish, alright?

t_ch: 那说来听听。
t_en: Well, okay then. I'm listening.

t_ch: 谢谢！我就知道，{NICKNAME}最好啦。
t_en: Thanks, {NICKNAME}, I knew it! You're the best!

t_ch: 「传说呀，在哀丽秘榭的秘境里，居住着一群小妖精。」
t_en: "According to legend, within the hidden depths of Aedes Elysiae, live a group of playful little fairies."

t_ch: 「妖精们与世隔绝，却向往人类的生活。它们想模仿村民，举办一场宴会，却唯独缺少了最重要的人。」
t_en: "The fairies, though hidden away from the world, dreamed of living like humans. They tried to hold a grand banquet, just as the villagers would. But one thing was missing: the most important person."

t_ch: 「它们等呀等，终于有一天，迷境入口传来了脚步声！妖精们纷纷竖起了耳朵……」
t_en: "They waited and waited... and then, one day, footsteps echoed from the secret entrance! The fairies all pricked up their ears in excitement..."

t_ch: 哎呀，你说…故事的主人公，现在是不是也竖起小耳朵了呢？
t_en: Hah, would you say the hero of our little story has their ears pricked up right now?

t_ch: 轻柔的睡意逐渐包裹住思绪，你坠入了最深沉的梦中……
t_en: A gentle drowsiness gradually envelops your thoughts, and you fall into the deepest of dreams...

t_ch: 迷！迷迷！
t_en: Mem! Mem, mem!!!
...Mem?

t_ch: 好灰的迷迷。
t_en: ...A really gray Mem.

t_ch: 我已经在故事里了？
t_en: I'm already in the story?

t_ch: 「竟然！主人公不仅察觉了世界的本质，还说出了非常不得了的台词。但人家必须指出，扮演上层叙事者是犯规的哦！」
t_en: "Incredible! The hero has uncovered the essence of the world... and even spoke a line that's truly beyond expectation! But I must remind you... taking on the narrator's role is against the rules!"

t_ch: 村长大人，这边！
t_en: Chief, over here!

t_ch: 是「英雄」！小涟为我们找来的「英雄」，终于到啦！
t_en: It's really the "hero"! The hero that Reney found for us has finally arrived!

t_ch: 呵呵。这位贵客，我对你的到来早有预感。
t_en: Heh. Welcome, dear guest. I sensed you would come.

t_ch: 当枝叶间的风铃第二十四次鸣响，蜜花丛中的粉蝶开启第四次绕行，我便知道。
t_en: When the wind chimes in the leaves rang for the twenty-fourth time, and the pink butterfly amidst the honey flowers took its fourth flight, I was certain.

t_ch: 拯救「迷路迷境」的贵客，终于出现了哪！
t_en: The honored guest who will save the Membrance Maze... has finally come!

t_ch: 村长，错了！客人在那边，那边！
t_en: Chief, no! The guest is over there. That way!

t_ch: 呵呵。我只是记性不太好，视力也有点差，但要做的事从来不会弄错。
t_en: Heh... Well, my memory's not the best, and my eyesight's a little fuzzy, but I never falter when it comes to doing the important stuff!

t_ch: 拯救「迷路迷境」的贵客，你终于出现了哪！
t_en: Honored guest who will save the Membrance Maze... you've finally arrived!

t_ch: 村长，是这边，这边……
t_en: Uh... Chief, over here...

t_ch: 要不说正事吧…
t_en: Let's just cut to the chase...

t_ch: 没关系，我无所谓。
t_en: It's fine. I'm not bothered.

t_ch: 真热闹呀，已经和妖精们打成一片了？不愧是{NICKNAME}，不管在哪里都魅力四射呢。
t_en: My, what a cheerful scene! Already getting along so well with the fairies? No surprise, {NICKNAME}. You shine no matter where you go.

t_ch: 村长，还有徕啦迷，好久不见！按照约定，我们来啦。
t_en: Chief, Relimem, it's been forever! But, we're here, just as we said we would be.

t_ch: 接下来要做什么？
t_en: What do we do next?

t_ch: 可以解释下前情吗？
t_en: Mind filling me in on the backstory?

t_ch: 你怎么也在故事里？
t_en: Wait, why are you in the story too?

t_ch: 「咳咳，这是故事的登场人物之一，出生在哀丽秘榭的平凡少女，只是恰好和我同名，又一样纯真可爱。」
t_en: "Ahem... this is one of the characters in the story, an ordinary girl born in Aedes Elysiae. She just so happens to have the same name as me... and is every bit as pure and adorable."

t_ch: 「体谅一下啦，即兴创作可是很难的。作为补偿，我会把你的部分讲得更绘声绘色哦！」
t_en: "Cut me some slack. Making up a story on the spot is really hard! But I promise, I'll make it up to you. I'll make your scenes as vivid and dramatic as possible!"

t_ch: 出于礼节，还是由我来说明吧。再次向各位贵客道谢，感谢你们——
t_en: It's only proper that I explain. Once more, I thank each and every honored guest. Thank you so much...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>特地前来迷路迷境，帮助妖精们重建荒废的餐馆。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>For coming to Membrance Maze, to help the fairies rebuild our long-abandoned restaurant.</color>

t_ch: 想吃美食，好想吃！迷！但是我们不会做饭！
t_en: We want tasty, scrumptious food, we really do! Mem! But we don't know how to cook!

t_ch: 求求了，英雄们！妖精们肚子瘪瘪的，就想毁灭世界！拜托了，请拯救迷路迷境！
t_en: Heroes, we're begging you! The fairies are starving... and when they're hungry, they just want to destroy the world! Please help us. Save the Membrance Maze!

t_ch: 原来是这种「拯救」吗？
t_en: I see... so this is the "saving" you had in mind?

t_ch: 真毁灭世界吗？
t_en: Actually "destroy the world"?

t_ch: 呵呵，徕啦迷说的夸张了。
t_en: Heh, Relimem was exaggerating just a smidgen.

t_ch: 哪有这么严重，顶多就是妖精们的怒火席卷七分之六个翁法罗斯。
t_en: It's not that serious... At most, their fury will burn through six-sevenths of Amphoreus.

t_ch: …那也在「严重」的范围内啦。
t_en: ...That still sounds pretty "serious" to me!

t_ch: 就是这样。就当是为了翁法罗斯，我们一起复兴餐馆，满足妖精们的小小愿望吧。
t_en: So that's the situation. For the sake of Amphoreus, let's rebuild the restaurant together and grant the fairies their little wish.

t_ch: 为了世界，义不容辞。
t_en: For the sake of the world, it's an obligation I gladly accept.

t_ch: 不明觉厉，姑且试试…
t_en: I have no idea what's going on, but it sounds cool... I'll give it a shot.

t_ch: 终于，终于…我得去向孩子们宣布，妖精们的餐馆，终于回来了哪！
t_en: At last... at long last! ! I have to tell the little ones that the fairies' restaurant is coming back at last!

t_ch: 指引就交给我吧。走，我先带你去看看…迷境里的小餐馆，别有一番情调呢。
t_en: Let me lead the way. Come on, I'll take you to see it... The little restaurant in the maze has a certain charm all its own.

t_ch: 这边这边，大家已经在布置场地啦。
t_en: This way, this way. Everyone's already setting things up.

t_ch: （大家？）
t_en: (Everyone?)

t_ch: 经营牵涉的琐碎杂务繁多，您当真要亲自承担统领一职？
t_en: Managing this place is bound to be full of endless trivial responsibilities... Do you really intend to personally shoulder the leadership role?

t_ch: 我既然接受邀请，来到妖精的居所，便当亲力亲为，否则不显得我空摆凯撒的架子，推辞重责么？
t_en: I already accepted the invitation and arrived in the fairies' home, so it's only right that I personally see to things. Otherwise, by avoiding my duties, wouldn't I be merely posing as Imperator?
